Package glide.api.models.commands

Class RangeOptions.RangeByLex

  • java.lang.Object
    • glide.api.models.commands.RangeOptions.RangeByLex
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    RangeOptions.RangeQuery
    Enclosing class:
    RangeOptions
    public static class RangeOptions.RangeByLex
extends java.lang.Object
implements RangeOptions.RangeQuery
    Represents a range by lexicographical order in a sorted set.
    The start and stop arguments represent lexicographical boundaries.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      RangeByLex​(RangeOptions.LexRange start, RangeOptions.LexRange end)
      Creates a range by lexicographical order in a sorted set.
      The start and stop arguments represent lexicographical boundaries.
      RangeByLex​(RangeOptions.LexRange start, RangeOptions.LexRange end, @NonNull RangeOptions.Limit limit)
      Creates a range by lexicographical order in a sorted set.
      The start and stop arguments represent lexicographical boundaries.

    • Method Summary

    • Constructor Detail

      • RangeByLex

        public RangeByLex​(@NonNull
                  RangeOptions.LexRange start,
                  @NonNull
                  RangeOptions.LexRange end,
                  @NonNull
                  @NonNull RangeOptions.Limit limit)
        Creates a range by lexicographical order in a sorted set.
        The start and stop arguments represent lexicographical boundaries.
        Parameters:
        start - The start lexicographic boundary.
        end - The stop lexicographic boundary.
        limit - The limit argument for a range query. Defaults to null. See Limit class for more information.

      • RangeByLex

        public RangeByLex​(@NonNull
                  RangeOptions.LexRange start,
                  @NonNull
                  RangeOptions.LexRange end)
        Creates a range by lexicographical order in a sorted set.
        The start and stop arguments represent lexicographical boundaries.
        Parameters:
        start - The start lexicographic boundary.
        end - The stop lexicographic boundary.
